sanskrit:
यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते।
सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथात्मा नोपलिप्यते॥
transliteration:
yathā sarva-gataṁ saukṣmyād ākāśaṁ nopalipyate |
sarvatrāvasthito dehe tathātmā nopalipyate ||
literal_meaning: As the all-pervading sky, due to its subtlety, is not tainted, so the Self, though situated everywhere in the body, is not tainted.
contextual_meaning: Your observing awareness can inhabit any thought, emotion or bodily sensation without becoming contaminated by it; like space holding a storm yet remaining only space.
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive distance—feeling the pain without being the pain.
trigger_scenario: You sit in therapy describing a panic attack while simultaneously noticing the part of you that is calmly observing the panic.
anchor_text: Witness every inner storm without the sky of you ever getting wet.
